message.content: Look at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues/427 and plan for a fix

content.content: {"body":"### Preflight Checklist - [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/moltisorg/moltis/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Abug) and this hasn't been reported yet - [x] I am using the latest version of Moltis - [x] If this happened during a chat session, I included as much full session context as possible and redacted secrets ### What happened? I mentioned this a little in https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues/360, but Qwen3-Coder-Next-FP8 continually gets confused when executing commands because of the `node` field. It always tries passing values before `null` and gets errors. I updated to the latest version today, but the problem persists. Here it took three attempts: ``` {"content":"Add The Traitors to the watchlist","created_at":1773425566977,"role":"user","run_id":"6cac6396-1ade-40c4-a679-0081fdcc5e82","seq":27} {"arguments":{"command":"cd /home/moltis/.moltis/skills/tv-watchlist && dart bin/main.dart add \"The Traitors\"","node":"","timeout":30,"working_dir":""},"created_at":1773425572332,"error":"node '' not found or not connected","role":"tool_result","success":false,"tool_call_id":"call_f84f11b01d37455e9199d1d0","tool_name":"exec"} {"arguments":{"command":"cd /home/moltis/.moltis/skills/tv-watchlist && dart bin/main.dart add \"The Traitors\"","node":"host","timeout":30,"working_dir":""},"created_at":1773425575237,"error":"node 'host' not found or not connected","role":"tool_result","success":false,"tool_call_id":"call_ccfe0988a5b64612912ba78b","tool_name":"exec"} {"arguments":{"command":"cd /home/moltis/.moltis/skills/tv-watchlist && dart bin/main.dart add \"The Traitors\"","node":null,"timeout":30,"working_dir":""},"created_at":1773425578892,"result":{"exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"Multiple matches found. Please re-run with an explicit ID using --id :\n- // snip ``` Given that I don't have any nodes, perhaps the best thing would be if the `node` parameter was just hidden from the tool description. Otherwise, perhaps the description could be clearer to try and avoid the model guessing what it should pass. ### Expected behavior The agent should have enough info to execute commands correctly first time, without multiple failed attempts. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Run moltis in a container with no extra nodes 2. Use `Qwen3-Coder-Next-FP8` (?).. I don't know if other models do this, but this one does it consistently for me. While it's not the smartest model, I don't think it's particularly bad either. 3. Do anything that involves using the `exec` command ### Did this happen during a chat session? Yes ### Chat session context (if applicable) ```markdown ``` ### Error messages / logs ```shell ``` ### Is this a regression? No, this never worked ### Last working version _No response_ ### Moltis version v0.10.18 ### Component Other ### Install method Docker ### Operating system Ubuntu / Debian ### Additional context _No response_","comments":[],"labels":[{"id":"LA_kwDOREW6tc8AAAACWauzgQ","name":"bug","description":"Something isn't working","color":"d73a4a"}],"state":"OPEN","title":"[Bug]: Agent repeatedly gets confused about nodes when executing commands"}

content.text: Now let me look at the exec tool schema and how the `node` parameter is defined.

input.prompt:
Find how the `exec` tool's schema/description is defined, specifically the `node` parameter. Look in crates/tools/ for the exec tool definition, its JSON schema, and how the `node` field is described to the LLM. Also check if there's any concept of "nodes" and how they're configured/detected. I need to understand:

1. Where the exec tool schema is built (the tool description sent to LLMs)
2. How the `node` parameter is defined in that schema
3. Where nodes are registered/checked (the "node not found" error path)
4. Whether there's a way to know at schema-build time if any nodes are configured

Be very thorough - check crates/tools/src/exec.rs, any schema builders, and the node/sandbox infrastructure.

content.text: Now let me check how the tool schema is provided to the LLM — whether it's built once or dynamically per request.

Good — `list_schemas()` calls `parameters_schema()` dynamically each time, so the schema is rebuilt per turn. The `has_connected_nodes()` check should work correctly at runtime.

Now I understand the full picture. There are **two problems**:

1. **Even when the node param is hidden from the schema, the model can still pass `node` and the execution path will process it.** Line 360-364 reads `node` from params regardless of whether it was in the schema. If the model hallucinates or guesses the parameter (e.g., empty string `""`), it gets processed.

2. **When `node` is passed as an empty string `""`, `v.as_str()` returns `Some("")`, which becomes `Some(String::from(""))`, and then the code tries to resolve node `""` — failing with "node '' not found".** The model also tries `"host"` which similarly fails.

Let me also check the user's scenario — they say "I don't have any nodes" but the `node` parameter is apparently showing up. This could mean:
- The schema IS being built with `node` (maybe `has_connected_nodes` is true even without real nodes), or  
- The model is hallucinating the parameter from prior context/training

Let me check if there's a `default_node` being set even without connected nodes.

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Let me enter plan mode.

Here's what I've found from analyzing the issue and codebase:

## Issue #427: Agent confused about `node` parameter in `exec` tool

The user (running Qwen3-Coder-Next-FP8 via OpenRouter, no remote nodes configured) sees the model repeatedly fail exec calls by passing `node: ""`, then `node: "host"`, before finally getting `node: null` right.

## Root Cause Analysis

There are **two separate problems**:

### 1. Schema: `node` may appear when it shouldn't

The `node` parameter is conditionally added to the schema via `has_connected_nodes()` (`exec.rs:321`), which checks an atomic counter. However, the `node_provider` is **always wired up** in `server.rs:3597-3606` — meaning `has_connected_nodes()` depends solely on the runtime `node_count` atomic. If a node connects then disconnects mid-session, the schema would flip. But more importantly, less capable models may pick up the `node` concept from the **system prompt** which includes `NODE_ROUTING_GUIDANCE` text (`prompt.rs:492-495`) whenever nodes are listed — this prompt section is gated on `has_nodes` from `PromptNodesRuntimeContext`, which is separate from the tool schema.

These two checks *should* be in sync, but they're separate code paths.

### 2. Execution: no guard against invalid `node` values from the model

Even when `node` is **not in the schema**, the execution path (`exec.rs:360-364`) still reads it from params. If a model hallucinates the parameter (empty string, `"host"`, etc.), the code processes it and fails with a confusing error. There's no filtering of empty strings or validation that the param was actually offered in the schema.

## Proposed Fix

**In `crates/tools/src/exec.rs`**, in the `execute()` method (~line 360):

1. **Filter out empty/whitespace-only `node` values** — treat `""` the same as `null`/absent
2. **Only attempt node routing if nodes are actually connected** — add a `has_connected_nodes()` guard before trying to resolve, so even if the model passes a `node` value when none exist, it's silently ignored and falls through to local execution
3. Improve the error message when node resolution fails to hint at valid values

This is a small, defensive change in one file. The schema-side gating already works correctly for the common case — the fix is hardening the execution path against model hallucinations.

Want me to proceed with this implementation?

content.text:
All clean. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Changes in `crates/tools/src/exec.rs`

**Two defensive fixes in the execution path (lines 362-411):**

1. **Filter empty/whitespace `node` values** — added `.filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty())` to the `node_ref` extraction chain. When a model passes `node: ""` or `node: "   "`, it's now treated the same as `node: null` (not specified).

2. **Guard against node routing when no nodes are connected** — added a `provider.has_connected_nodes()` check before attempting `resolve_node_id()`. When no nodes are connected, the node parameter is silently ignored with a `debug!` log and execution falls through to local/sandbox path. This prevents the confusing "node 'host' not found or not connected" error when models hallucinate the parameter.

**Four new tests:**
- `test_exec_ignores_node_param_when_no_nodes_connected` — bogus `node: "host"` falls through to local exec
- `test_exec_ignores_empty_node_param` — `node: ""` falls through
- `test_exec_ignores_whitespace_only_node_param` — `node: "   "` falls through
- `test_exec_schema_hides_node_when_no_nodes_connected` — schema doesn't include `node` when disconnected

input.command:
git add crates/tools/src/exec.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(tools): ignore exec node param when no nodes are connected (#427)

Models (especially weaker ones like Qwen3-Coder) hallucinate values for
the `node` parameter even when it is hidden from the tool schema,
causing repeated "node not found" errors before eventually passing null.

Two defensive changes:
- Filter empty/whitespace-only node strings so `""` is treated as absent
- Guard node routing behind `has_connected_nodes()` so hallucinated node
  values silently fall through to local execution when no nodes exist
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(tools): ignore exec node param when no nodes are connected" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Fixes #427 — models (especially weaker ones like Qwen3-Coder) hallucinate values for the `node` parameter on the `exec` tool even when it's hidden from the schema, causing repeated "node not found" errors before eventually passing `null`.

- Filter empty/whitespace-only `node` strings so `""` or `"   "` are treated as absent
- Guard node routing behind `has_connected_nodes()` so hallucinated node values silently fall through to local execution when no nodes exist
- Add 4 tests covering empty, whitespace, bogus node values, and schema hiding

## Validation

### Completed
- [x] `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all -- --check`
- [x] `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 clippy -p moltis-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- [x] `cargo test -p moltis-tools -- exec::tests` (35 tests pass)

### Remaining
- [ ] `./scripts/local-validate.sh`
- [ ] Full `cargo test`

## Manual QA

1. Run moltis with no remote nodes configured
2. Use a model that tends to hallucinate the `node` param (e.g. Qwen3-Coder-Next-FP8)
3. Ask it to execute a command — should succeed on first attempt without node errors
EOF
)"

content.content: https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/474

content.text: PR created: https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/474

message.content: Look at comment from https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/474 fix and resolve discussions

content.text: Good feedback — the `default_node` case should error when nodes are disconnected since the admin explicitly configured remote execution. Let me fix both issues.

content.content: The file /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/grizzled-climb/crates/tools/src/exec.rs has been updated successfully.

lines: // to that node. Otherwise fall through to local/sandbox execution.
// Filter empty/whitespace-only strings — some models pass `""` when they
// don't know what value to use, which should be treated as "not specified".
-        // Only attempt node routing when nodes are actually connected.  If no
-        // nodes are available the param is silently ignored and execution falls
-        // through to the local/sandbox path, avoiding confusing errors when the
+        // Determine the effective node reference, distinguishing model-supplied
+        // values from the admin-configured default.  When no nodes are connected:
+        // - Model-hallucinated values are silently dropped (fall through to local).
+        // - A configured `default_node` produces a clear error so the admin knows
+        //   the intended remote host is unavailable.
-                    Error::message(format!("node '{node_ref}' not found or not connected"))
+            } else if let Some(ref dn) = self.default_node {
+                    "default node '{dn}' is configured but no nodes are currently connected"
+                    debug!("ignoring model-supplied node parameter — no nodes are connected");
+                Error::message(format!("node '{node_ref}' not found or not connected"))
-            debug!(node_ref, "ignoring node parameter — no nodes are connected");
// Check sandbox state early — we need it for working_dir resolution.

content.text: Now update the tests — add the `default_node` test and update existing ones since the structure changed slightly:

+            "error should explain no nodes are connected, got: {msg}"
